refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use by minimizing data acquisition latency and processing latency.
refined_fr.rationale: To meet the overall latency requirement, the update process is decomposed into two key sub-functions: acquiring timely data from the worksite and processing that data efficiently to update the model.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure latency separately for data acquisition and data processing stages under representative operational scenarios to ensure combined latency meets p95 ≤ 1.0 s.
sub_frs: FR1: Acquire worksite data with minimal latency
FR2: Process acquired data to update the worksite model within latency constraints

candidate_dps: DP1: Use high-frequency telemetry data streams with prioritized network scheduling
DP2: Implement edge computing for local data pre-processing to reduce central processing load
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Use high-frequency telemetry data streams with prioritized network scheduling

potential_sources: Telemetry data dependency between acquisition and processing
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically separates data acquisition (high-frequency telemetry) and processing, minimizing coupling by prioritizing network scheduling to meet latency.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network link supports prioritized scheduling and sufficient bandwidth
Risk of telemetry data loss or delay impacting update latency
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider adding edge computing (DP2) if network constraints cause latency violations.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system responsible for telemetry data acquisition, processing, and worksite model update

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives high-frequency telemetry data from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports prioritized data transmission for telemetry and tasking

module_containment_scores.notes: Centralizes telemetry data acquisition and processing, minimizing diffusion.
Primarily data source; limited containment of update logic.
Supports telemetry but not responsible for update processing.
No direct role in worksite model update latency.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing the update logic and telemetry data handling within the NGHE Solution module reduces latency and interface complexity.
containment_risks: Potential bottleneck if NGHE Solution module becomes overloaded
Dependency on network link performance may still cause latency spikes
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Monitor NGHE Solution module load and consider distributing processing if latency targets are not met.

fr_dp_gate.reason: FR refinement clearly decomposes latency requirement into acquisition and processing sub-functions; selected DP addresses acquisition with prioritized telemetry streams.

diffusion_risks: Low diffusion risk as update logic is centralized in NGHE Solution module
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support NGHE Solution as best ownership candidate for DP implementation.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by measuring telemetry latency and processing times within NGHE Solution module using known interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of FR to DP and IM with well-defined interfaces (Telemetry, Network Link).

recommended_revisions: Evaluate edge computing DP if latency targets are not met in integration testing.
Assess network link performance impact on telemetry data delivery.

decision.justification: Current level of decomposition and assignments meet the latency requirement with clear ownership and manageable risks; no immediate need to zigzag or revise.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Acquire worksite data with minimal latency
FR2: Process acquired data to update the worksite model within latency constraints

revise.why: Consider edge computing DP if network constraints cause latency violations in future iterations.

assumptions: Network link supports prioritized scheduling and sufficient bandwidth
Telemetry data is reliable and timely
open_risks: Potential overload of NGHE Solution module
evidence_needed: Latency measurements of telemetry data acquisition and processing
Network link performance statistics under operational load
